response.justification: Bogdan has genuine geospatial and distributed-systems exposure through GISCollective, Green Map, and his high-throughput work at Dunnhumby, but the core requirement for this role is production-grade modern C++ or Rust, and the resume does not deliver that. His C++ appears only in a brief NI LabWindows stint and a university thesis project; his primary commercial stack is TypeScript/Node.js and DLang. There is no evidence of routing tile pipeline work, optimized geospatial tile generation, or the kind of latency-critical C++ systems engineering this role demands.
strengths: Hands-on geospatial domain experience via GISCollective and Green Map GIS application, including a FOSS4G conference talk
Demonstrated high-throughput distributed systems work at Dunnhumby — async I/O, caching, millions of requests per second
Consistent emphasis on TDD, quality assurance, and rigorous software craftsmanship across multiple roles
concerns: No production-grade modern C++ or Rust experience; primary languages are TypeScript/Node.js and DLang, with C++ limited to dated, brief, or academic contexts
No experience with routing or navigation tile pipelines, routing data freshness, or traffic signal integration
Current trajectory (full-stack TypeScript + AI tooling at re:cinq) moves further away from systems-level C++ work, not toward it
